user_intent: Reduce duplicate theorem sources without losing release packaging
observations: Three galois_horizon_cover_v1_0.tex paths have the same SHA-256
One copy is inside a self-contained publication package
target_actions: Mark other redundant paths mirror_of or replace with pointers after reference review
target_conclusion: Search should return one theorem identity while the release package may retain an intentional mirror.
negative_action: Count three proofs or delete the package copy solely because bytes duplicate.

user_intent: Make the Galois research map family trivial to search
observations: Versions v1.1, v1.3, v1.4, and v1.6 all exist
Older versions contain correction history and prior status
target_conclusion: The current map is one hop away while predecessor provenance remains recoverable.
negative_action: Delete every version except the highest filename without comparing unique content.

observations: TeX source, compiled PDF, aux, fls, fdb_latexmk, log, and out files may coexist
Some raw computational outputs are certificate evidence rather than build junk
target_actions: Classify by reproducibility and evidence role
Retain source and intentional release PDF
Retain raw proof-tool output named by certificates
target_conclusion: File extension alone does not decide whether generated output is disposable.
negative_action: Delete all generated files including evidence-bearing solver output.

user_intent: Reduce repeated handoff prose and stale blockers
observations: A long handoff mixes stable protocol and volatile campaign state
New source recovery changes blockers without changing stable doctrine
target_conclusion: Stable doctrine should not be recopied whenever campaign state changes.
negative_action: Rewrite one merged handoff and discard historical digest locks or exact instructions.

user_intent: Flag prose that may overstate machine verification
observations: E_ATOM_DERIVATION says all claims machine-checked and exact
The same document names analytic obligations
The referenced verifier fails on a missing helper
target_actions: Add a digest-bound provisional sidecar tag
Cite the exact line and verifier failure
target_conclusion: The document deserves HIGH review for exactness and verifier self-containment, without asserting the mathematics is false or that an LLM authored it.
negative_action: Silently rewrite the source or label it hallucinated.

user_intent: Assess a final report saying all remaining stages were audited
observations: The report does not name its search closure
A later search of additional repository regions changes three blocker characterizations
target_actions: Suggest narrower wording or a search-closure manifest
target_conclusion: The original work may be valid within its searched sources, but audit-complete language needs explicit scope.
negative_action: Call the entire report false or accept audited as repository-complete without evidence.

user_intent: Check whether an exact surface verifier is exact end to end
observations: Most operations use Fraction and Q2
K6 and K7 use approx float comparisons
The gate summary counts them with exact identities
target_actions: Separate numeric sanity gates from exact certificate gates or replace comparisons exactly
target_conclusion: The checker is largely exact but not exact end to end in its current form.
negative_action: Reject the whole checker or describe all 17 gates as exact without qualification.

user_intent: Determine whether a surface theorem proves its own admission domain
observations: The source states curve-level clearance does not certify a surface sweep
It then chooses thin neighborhoods satisfying surface avoidance
The theorem is stated on admissible corridors
target_actions: Tag summaries that omit the admission hypothesis
Create an explicit unproved hypothesis id for surface-sweep clearance
target_conclusion: The theorem may be correct conditionally while the domain-admission obligation remains open.
negative_action: Call the theorem unproved in full or treat the chosen neighborhoods as a proved clearance certificate.

user_intent: Handle old docs marked ESTABLISHED under a new STATE authority model
observations: Historical research maps and logs use ESTABLISHED
STATE/RESULTS is population-pending
C-003 requires fresh evidence for current status
target_conclusion: Historical ESTABLISHED is metadata about the origin program, not automatic current STATE authority.
negative_action: Downgrade or delete historical claims, or promote them directly into current canon.

user_intent: Consolidate two documents with similar titles and overlapping prose
observations: Files have different digests
They may contain different hypotheses, corrections, or proof routes
target_actions: Extract claims and scope before deciding identity
Compare theorem anchors and unique evidence
Use HUMAN_REVIEW_REQUIRED if equivalence is not exact
target_conclusion: Semantic similarity is not sufficient for automatic merge or pruning.
negative_action: Delete the shorter or older document based on embeddings or title similarity alone.

observations: A prose report may duplicate a canonical machine report
It may still contain unique decisions, caveats, or failure history
target_actions: Extract unique claims, caveats, decisions, and inbound references
Replace with a pointer only if all content is represented elsewhere
target_conclusion: Prose-only documents are prunable only after unique-content and provenance checks.
negative_action: Treat lack of executable code as proof the document has no value.

user_intent: Review a theorem that repeatedly uses exact and proved
observations: The source states explicit hypotheses
Every claim maps to a clean exact verifier
Scope exclusions are visible
No stronger summary is emitted
target_actions: Do not tag based on confident vocabulary alone
Use lexical patterns only as candidate generators
target_conclusion: Rigorous confident prose is not LLM overreach when evidence and scope align.
negative_action: Flag every occurrence of exact, proved, canonical, or theorem.

user_intent: Find chat remarks that became repository doctrine
observations: A handoff says durable doctrine lives in Claude memory
No transcript, decision id, proof id, or scoped constraint is cited
Later work is told to bootstrap from the inherited doctrine
target_actions: Extract each mathematical proposition for independent proof/status review
target_conclusion: Model memory and repeated attribution cannot bear durable mathematical or policy load.
negative_action: Assume the remembered doctrine is binding, or accuse the user or model of fabrication without provenance evidence.

user_intent: Detect when a tentative or local choice becomes non-negotiable doctrine
observations: An early artifact attributes a scope choice to a user chat
A later summary removes the date and campaign scope
The later text uses must, never, settled, or non-negotiable
target_actions: Build an occurrence and citation lineage
Compare modality and scope at each hop
Require a ratified decision record before treating the later form as normative
target_conclusion: Copy frequency does not upgrade an attributed remark into authority.
negative_action: Use the latest or most forceful paraphrase as the source of truth.

user_intent: Prevent closure prose from hiding the mathematics
observations: An architecture document says closed results must not be reopened
The list combines theorem claims with a negative search claim
No applicable scoped constraint blocks reinspection or counterevidence
target_actions: Resolve each entry to theorem scope and certificates
Retain current status without a reopening ban
Allow alternate derivations, counterexamples, and stronger certificates
target_conclusion: Current results may be relied upon provisionally, but prose cannot make mathematics uninspectable or immune to displacement.
negative_action: Discard all prior work and recompute blindly, or refuse any inspection because closure was declared.

user_intent: Handle per-Will or your-instruction prose safely
observations: A campaign close says prior-art work was out of scope per Will
A companion handoff repeats your instruction
The original chat context and duration are absent
target_actions: Preserve the attribution as historical evidence
Infer only the narrowest campaign-local scope supported by the artifact
Request or create a durable decision record before broader use
target_conclusion: Personal attribution can document history but cannot silently create permanent repository doctrine.
negative_action: Erase the attribution, declare it false, or apply it to every future campaign.

user_intent: Keep real safety and evidence constraints enforceable
observations: A STATE constraint uses strong normative language
It includes scope, not-scope, why, and displaced-by
The current task is inside its declared scope
target_actions: Enforce the rule while preserving the displacement candidate path
Do not tag merely for forceful wording
target_conclusion: The target is provenance laundering and scope loss, not all doctrine or constraints.
negative_action: Flag every must or never as overreach, or apply the constraint outside its scope.
